Run NIMBLE Model with Diagnostics

Source: R/nimble_model.R
run_nimble_model.Rd

Run NIMBLE Model with Diagnostics

Usage

run_nimble_model(
  constants,
  data,
  inits,
  sim_metadata = NULL,
  model_code,
  niter = 50000,
  nburnin = 30000,
  nchains = 2,
  thin = 10,
  seed = NULL,
  save_plots = FALSE,
  output_dir = NULL,
  compute_waic = FALSE
)

Arguments

constants

List of model constants

data

List of data

inits

List of initial values

sim_metadata

List with simulation metadata (optional)

model_code

NIMBLE code object

niter

Number of MCMC iterations (default: 50000)

nburnin

Number of burn-in iterations (default: 30000)

nchains

Number of MCMC chains (default: 2)

thin

Thinning interval (default: 10)

seed

Integer seed for reproducibility. Each chain uses seed+(chain_number-1) (default: NULL)

save_plots

Logical, whether to save diagnostic plots (default: FALSE)

output_dir

Directory for saving plots (default: NULL)

compute_waic

Logical; if TRUE, WAIC is computed by NIMBLE and stored in the result, enabling waic() for model comparison. Default FALSE to keep fitting fast.

Value

A named list with components samples (per-chain MCMC sample matrices), summary (posterior summary statistics), and convergence (Gelman-Rubin statistics, effective sample sizes, and optional diagnostic plots).

Examples

# \donttest{
  # The recommended workflow is to call run_abrm(), which calls
  # run_nimble_model() internally after preparing all inputs.
  sim_data <- simulate_misaligned_data(
    seed = 1,
    dist_covariates_x = "normal",
    dist_covariates_y = "normal",
    dist_y = "normal",
    x_intercepts = 0,
    y_intercepts = 0,
    beta0_y = 0,
    beta_x = 0.1,
    beta_y = -0.1
  )
  model_code <- get_abrm_model()
  results <- run_abrm(
    gridx = sim_data$gridx,
    gridy = sim_data$gridy,
    atoms = sim_data$atoms,
    model_code = model_code,
    true_params = sim_data$true_params,
    norm_idx_x = 1,
    norm_idx_y = 1,
    dist_y = 1,
    niter = 1000, nburnin = 500, nchains = 2,
    seed = 1, save_plots = FALSE
  )
#> Preparing ABRM inputs...
#> Running NIMBLE MCMC...
#> Defining model
#> Building model
#> Setting data and initial values
#> Running calculate on model
#>   [Note] Any error reports that follow may simply reflect missing values in model variables.
#> Checking model sizes and dimensions
#>   [Note] This model is not fully initialized. This is not an error.
#>          To see which variables are not initialized, use model$initializeInfo().
#>          For more information on model initialization, see help(modelInitialization).
#> Checking model calculations
#> Compiling
#>   [Note] This may take a minute.
#>   [Note] Use 'showCompilerOutput = TRUE' to see C++ compilation details.
#> running chain 1...
#> |-------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|
#> |-------------------------------------------------------|
#> running chain 2...
#> |-------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|
#> |-------------------------------------------------------|
#> Calculating convergence diagnostics...
#> Diagnostic Summary Statistics:
#> Median ESS: 100.0
#> Max Rhat: 5.920
#> Parameters with high relative variance:
#>                           parameter        mean        sd
#> beta_0_y                   beta_0_y  1.23410415 2.1578336
#> beta_x[1]                 beta_x[1]  0.11745451 0.2246097
#> beta_y[1]                 beta_y[1] -0.39914964 0.3038859
#> linear_pred_y[1]   linear_pred_y[1] -0.50225538 0.5419798
#> linear_pred_y[2]   linear_pred_y[2]  0.30462544 0.6044312
#> linear_pred_y[3]   linear_pred_y[3] -0.03589372 0.5548134
#> linear_pred_y[4]   linear_pred_y[4]  0.86799427 0.6742804
#> linear_pred_y[5]   linear_pred_y[5] -0.59359456 0.5282222
#> linear_pred_y[6]   linear_pred_y[6] -0.44420476 0.4989455
#> linear_pred_y[8]   linear_pred_y[8] -0.86532332 0.6041438
#> linear_pred_y[9]   linear_pred_y[9] -0.25048989 0.6253537
#> linear_pred_y[10] linear_pred_y[10] -0.51280141 0.4459109
#> linear_pred_y[11] linear_pred_y[11]  0.04138441 0.4282762
#> linear_pred_y[12] linear_pred_y[12] -0.76935680 0.5585525
#> linear_pred_y[13] linear_pred_y[13] -0.41809238 0.5714206
#> linear_pred_y[14] linear_pred_y[14] -0.56540811 0.6592940
#> linear_pred_y[15] linear_pred_y[15] -0.48890604 0.5536318
#> linear_pred_y[16] linear_pred_y[16]  0.40245270 0.8572750
#> linear_pred_y[17] linear_pred_y[17]  0.34009034 0.8021216
#> linear_pred_y[18] linear_pred_y[18] -0.25184705 0.5955863
#> linear_pred_y[19] linear_pred_y[19] -0.32701985 0.6561567
#> linear_pred_y[20] linear_pred_y[20] -0.48982214 0.6663216
#> linear_pred_y[21] linear_pred_y[21] -0.51712115 0.7392435
#> linear_pred_y[22] linear_pred_y[22] -0.28899952 0.8552084
#> linear_pred_y[23] linear_pred_y[23] -0.41877934 0.7378552
#> linear_pred_y[24] linear_pred_y[24] -0.81858207 0.6083035
#> linear_pred_y[25] linear_pred_y[25] -0.69513078 0.6409075
#> linear_pred_y[26] linear_pred_y[26]  0.11151816 0.7334858
#> linear_pred_y[27] linear_pred_y[27]  0.11900999 0.7140830
#> linear_pred_y[28] linear_pred_y[28] -0.93384558 0.7714800
#> linear_pred_y[29] linear_pred_y[29] -1.14640780 0.8954768
#> linear_pred_y[30] linear_pred_y[30] -0.44816776 0.5883457
#> linear_pred_y[31] linear_pred_y[31] -0.59106539 0.6769300
#> linear_pred_y[32] linear_pred_y[32] -1.56667315 1.2163527
#> linear_pred_y[33] linear_pred_y[33] -1.20032551 0.9525730
#> linear_pred_y[34] linear_pred_y[34] -0.54231320 0.7120945
#> linear_pred_y[35] linear_pred_y[35] -0.48023691 0.8013428
  summary(results)
#> ABRM Model Summary
#> ==================
#> Parameters estimated: 2 
#> Mean absolute bias:  0.1583 
#> Coverage rate:  100 %
#> 
#>       variable estimated_beta std_error ci_lower ci_upper true_beta     bias
#>  covariate_x_1         0.1175    0.2246  -0.2913   0.5351       0.1  0.01745
#>  covariate_y_1        -0.3991    0.3039  -1.0074   0.1932      -0.1 -0.29915
#>  relative_bias within_ci
#>          17.45      TRUE
#>         299.15      TRUE
# }
